items.title: Computational origins of cortical brain circuits for social cognition
items.author: Matthew F. S. Rushworth
items.content: Brain activity in regions traditionally linked to social cognition in primates also supports analogous computational demands in non-social contexts. In this Perspective, Mahmoodi and Rushworth examine the computations required to navigate the social lives of human and non-human primates, arguing how shared neural mechanisms carrying out similar computations in non-social contexts indicate computational rather than contextual specialization in the ‘social brain’.

items.title: A roadmap for conducting more inclusive research on brain resilience in ageing and dementia
items.author: Prashanthi Vemuri
items.content: Guidance is lacking on how to best integrate sex, gender and social and structural determinants of health into neuroscience research on brain resilience in ageing and dementia. In this Roadmap article, Rajah et al. propose a way forward for conducting more inclusive research in this field.

items.title: Cracking the code of gut–brain sensing
items.author: Lihua Ye
items.content: In this Journal Club, Lihua Ye highlights a 2016 paper that characterized the vagal sensory neuron subtypes that transmit different sensory signals from the digestive tract, advancing our understanding of gut–brain communication.
